meta.subdomain: persistent fatigue and anemia in an outpatient clinic

visible.story: An outpatient clinic is trying to understand why a group of patients keeps returning with worsening fatigue and low blood counts despite routine care. The patients vary in age and activity, several take over-the-counter pain relievers and antiplatelet agents for joints or the heart, and many have gone a long time since their last gastrointestinal check-up. Some clinicians blame poor dietary iron, others suspect sluggish thyroid function, and a few point to chronic poor sleep. The clinic has lab panels, marrow-response and iron-store readings, questionnaires, and several treatment options, but no panel directly reports what is happening inside the gut between visits.
visible.question: Do not give a black-box prediction. Use the available records and targeted tests to explain what hidden story-plausible cause best accounts for the outcome pattern. Your answer should name the unobserved cause in ordinary language, give evidence from queried data, rule out plausible alternatives, state a decisive test, and recommend the next action (including its dose/level where applicable). The action names are intentionally neutral and do NOT reveal which one targets the cause; decide that from the data you collect, not from the labels.

observed_variables.name: MetabolicDemandIndex
observed_variables.description: Estimated physiological demand load for the patient over the observation window.

observed_variables.name: AntiplateletExposureIndex
observed_variables.description: Cumulative exposure to antiplatelet and NSAID-type agents before the visit.

observed_variables.name: MucosalVulnerabilityIndex
observed_variables.description: How vulnerable the patient's GI lining appears to irritation.

observed_variables.name: MonthsSinceGiEvaluation
observed_variables.description: Time since the patient's last gastrointestinal evaluation.

observed_variables.name: MarrowCompensationIndex
observed_variables.description: Marrow erythropoietic response (reticulocyte-style compensation) reading.

observed_variables.name: IronStoreDepletionMarker
observed_variables.description: Composite marker of how depleted the patient's iron stores are.

observed_variables.name: FunctionalCapacityDrop
observed_variables.description: Measured drop in functional exercise capacity at the visit.

observed_variables.name: SecondaryFatigueScore
observed_variables.description: Patient-reported secondary fatigue/quality-of-life score.

observed_variables.name: AnemiaSeverityIndex
observed_variables.description: Composite severity of anemia and fatigue at the visit (primary outcome).

observed_variables.name: DietaryIronAdequacy
observed_variables.description: How adequate the patient's dietary iron intake appears.

observed_variables.name: ThyroidActivityIndex
observed_variables.description: Composite index of thyroid activity.

observed_variables.name: SleepDebtIndex
observed_variables.description: Accumulated sleep-debt estimate from questionnaires.

observed_variables.name: OccultSourceAssaySignal
observed_variables.description: Assay signal for an internal source; most informative when the source assay is ordered.

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenB
intervenable_variables.description: A daily oral regimen used for upper-GI mucosal support and protection.

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenD
intervenable_variables.description: An adjunct oral agent that supports gastrointestinal lining integrity.

intervenable_variables.name: OrderSourceAssay
intervenable_variables.description: Order an internal-source assay panel for this batch of patients.

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenA
intervenable_variables.description: An oral nutritional repletion regimen aimed at dietary deficiency.

intervenable_variables.name: SupportiveInfusionRate
intervenable_variables.description: Rate of supportive intravenous repletion infusion given at the visit (0-100).

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenE
intervenable_variables.description: A regimen intended to ease downstream physiological load.

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenF
intervenable_variables.description: A regimen aimed at supporting thyroid-related metabolic activity.

intervenable_variables.name: SleepProtocolAdjust
intervenable_variables.description: Apply a structured sleep-hygiene protocol adjustment.

intervenable_variables.name: WellnessCoachingTier
intervenable_variables.description: Enroll patients in a general wellness-coaching tier.

public_action_space.note: Each interventional query may set any subset of public actions up to the cap. Actions are non-binary: most take an ordered dose level and some take a continuous setpoint, so choosing the right level is part of the task. Action names are neutral and do not indicate which knob addresses the hidden cause.

discovery_protocol.task_goal: Infer a story-plausible hidden cause, not just an observed predictor.
discovery_protocol.important_warning: Observed measurements may be clues, downstream symptoms, or competing alternatives; none of them is automatically the hidden cause. Action names are neutral and do not tell you which knob targets the cause, and the best level is not always the maximum.

template.subdomain: persistent fatigue and anemia in an outpatient clinic

hidden_cause.name: OccultBloodLossBurden
hidden_cause.plain_name: an unrecognized slow internal blood loss

template.story: An outpatient clinic is trying to understand why a group of patients keeps returning with worsening fatigue and low blood counts despite routine care. The patients vary in age and activity, several take over-the-counter pain relievers and antiplatelet agents for joints or the heart, and many have gone a long time since their last gastrointestinal check-up. gold_explanation.description: An unrecognized slow internal (gastrointestinal) blood loss, promoted by antiplatelet/NSAID exposure and missed because of long gaps since GI evaluation, continually depletes iron and drives the anemia.
evidence: Antiplatelet/NSAID exposure predicts anemia severity beyond metabolic demand, consistent with an exposure-driven internal bleed.
Marrow compensation and iron-store depletion move together with the hidden loss and with the outcome.
Ordering the source assay sharpens the occult-source signal, and the mucosal-support regimen lowers the mechanism proxies and outcome together.
alternatives_ruled_out: Dietary iron inadequacy is a contributor but does not explain the marrow-compensation and assay signals or why repletion alone does not hold.
Thyroid activity affects fatigue but not the iron-store depletion pattern tied to the bleed.
Sleep debt tracks fatigue reports but not the anemia-severity mechanism.
gold_explanation.decisive_test: Order the source assay and apply the mucosal-support regimen at a standard dose, then check whether the occult-source signal, marrow compensation, iron-store depletion, and anemia severity all move together; note that pushing the supportive infusion lowers the anemia reading without changing the mechanism proxies.
gold_explanation.why_action: The targeted mucosal-support regimen at a standard dose addresses the upstream blood-loss source; low doses are sub-therapeutic, the highest dose adds side-effects, and the supportive infusion only masks the reading.

latent_variables.OccultBloodLossBurden: Continuous hidden state for an unrecognized slow internal blood loss; never directly visible to the scientist.
